title: newyorkfed / staff report / term premium | Research Paper | Rate-Amplifying Demand and the Excess Sensitivity of Long-Te...
summary: This Federal Reserve Bank of New York Staff Report (No. 810, revised April 2021) by Hanson, Lucca, and Wright documents that long-term nominal interest rates exhibit excess sensitivity to high-frequency movements in short-term rates, and that this sensitivity has strengthened since 2000. The researchers propose a model where declines in short rates trigger rate-amplifying demand shifts in long-term bonds that are met with limited and slow arbitrage, causing long rates to temporarily overreact to short rate changes.
